vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Find cap at side of console shifter. Remove.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: 4WD: transfer case MUST be in 2H before towing on road.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from front (rear/drive wheels roll on ground). Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button (NV244/NV246 Autotrac) or manual lever depending on trim
positions: 2WD (2H) — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi (4H) — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo (4L) — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
ELECTRIC: Press the 2WD button on the dash. Wait for 2WD indicator.
MANUAL: Stop completely. Shift TC lever to 2H.
Confirm 2WD indicator is illuminated before lifting rear wheels.
NEVER tow in 4H, 4WD Auto, or 4L.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — trim panel near selector, remove to expose slot
shift: Locate trim panel near electronic selector. Remove or pry.
Insert key into slot, press down.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from front (rear/drive wheels roll on ground). Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric rotary dial on center console
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD engagement — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD high — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Turn the rotary TC dial on the center console to 2WD.
Confirm the 2WD indicator lamp on the instrument cluster.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: eTorque Mild Hybrid — Not Full Hybrid
knownIssues.detail: The eTorque system is a 48V mild hybrid assist. Despite having a small lithium battery, this is NOT a full hybrid. The 12V battery under the hood is used for jumping normally. The eTorque battery is not accessible for jumping and is managed separately by the vehicle.

battery.warnings: eTorque 48V system is separate — 12V under hood for jumping only.

neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: High-performance off-road trim. Same as DT 1500 procedure.
vehicles.towMethod: Transfer case to 2H first, then lift from front (rear/drive wheels roll on ground), or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric rotary dial on center console
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER on pavement
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Turn TC dial to 2WD.
Confirm indicator.
Safe to lift rear wheels.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Find cap at side of console shifter. Remove.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: Cummins diesel: same procedure. 4WD standard. Heavy-duty.
vehicles.warn: 4WD: transfer case. Heavy-duty — use heavy wheel blocks.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Heavy-duty — use rated equipment. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever (NV271) or Electric push-button (NV273) depending on trim
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position on road
steps: MANUAL: Stop completely. Shift TC floor lever firmly to 2H.
ELECTRIC: Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2H button. Confirm indicator.
Confirm 2H indicator before lifting rear wheels.
NEVER tow in 4H on dry pavement or in 4L.
Heavy-duty — use properly rated chains, straps, and wheel blocks.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
DIESEL: Same shift-lock and tow procedures as gasoline variant. Heavier curb weight — account for extra weight in equipment ratings.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: Manual Transfer Case Lever May Be Stiff
knownIssues.detail: The NV271 manual transfer case floor lever on older HD Rams can be extremely stiff, especially if the vehicle has been in 4WD for extended periods. Rock the vehicle gently in neutral while applying firm pressure to the lever. Do not force — damage to the TC shift fork can occur.

knownIssues.title: Very Heavy Vehicle — Equipment Ratings Critical
knownIssues.detail: Ram 2500/3500 curb weight ranges from 6,400–7,200 lbs. A fully loaded 3500 dually can exceed 14,000 lbs GVWR. Verify all wheel-lift and flatbed equipment is rated appropriately. Standard equipment is often under-rated for these trucks.

battery.location: Under hood. Cummins diesel may have dual batteries on driver side.
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red (+) on either battery (diesel), black (−) to engine block
battery.warnings: Diesel dual batteries. Very heavy. Verify equipment ratings. Manual TC lever may be stiff on older models.

vehicles.slot: Column shifter — slot on steering column; OR console slot on some trims
shift: COLUMN: Find slot on steering column. Insert key, press down. Press brake. Move to N.
CONSOLE: Find slot at console shifter base. Insert key, press down. Press brake. Move to N.
neutral: Connect jump pack. Shift normally or use slot.
vehicles.notes: Commercial van. Based on Fiat Ducato platform.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear (front/drive wheels roll on ground) or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
